title: Institution Rankings
subtitle: Government institutions ranked by procurement risk and vendor concentration — click column headers to sort
tableDescription: {{count}} institutions. Click column headers to sort. Click an institution name to view its profile.

columns.concentrationTooltip: Vendor Concentration: 1.0 = single vendor, 0.0 = perfectly competitive

publicationDelay.title: Publication Delay Transparency
publicationDelay.description: Days between procedure publication and contract award. Shorter windows compress vendor preparation time and favour pre-selected suppliers.

heatmapPage.title: Institution × Sector Risk Heatmap
heatmapPage.description: Average risk score for each of the top 20 institutions (by contract count) across all 12 procurement sectors. Green = low risk, amber = moderate, red = high risk. Click any cell to explore the contracts for that institution and sector.
heatmapPage.clickHint: Click a cell to explore those contracts in the Contracts view with institution and sector pre-filtered.
heatmapPage.errorDesc: The institution rankings or sector data could not be fetched. Please try refreshing.
heatmapPage.footnote: Values show average risk score (0–100%). Click any cell to view filtered contracts.

health.headline: Institutions Under Vendor Control
health.subtitle: An analysis of the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index reveals which agencies have been captured by a single vendor
health.lede: When a single company wins more than 50% of an agency's contracts, experts call it institutional capture. In Mexico, {{count}} agencies exhibit this pattern, concentrating {{amount}} in public spending under the control of a handful of vendors.
health.top3Subtitle: The agencies where a single vendor has the most absolute control over public spending.
health.rankingsSubtitle: Click headers to sort. Red-bordered rows indicate capture (HHI ≥ 0.50); orange border indicates high concentration.
health.impactText: Institutional capture in healthcare means essential medicines are procured from a single supplier, eliminating competition and raising prices. In infrastructure, it means public works are awarded without real competition. In every case, the citizen pays more for less.
health.loadError: Could not load data. Try reloading the page.

methodology.label: Methodology
methodology.question: What is the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index and why does it matter?
methodology.pullQuote: An HHI of 1.0 means all of an agency's money goes to a single supplier. It is the statistical definition of institutional capture.
methodology.body1: The Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI) measures how concentrated spending is within an institution. It is calculated by summing the squares of each vendor's market share. An HHI near 0 indicates high competition; an HHI of 1.0 indicates absolute monopoly.
methodology.body2: In public contracts, extreme concentration is a red flag: it suggests a vendor has eliminated competition, whether through legitimate capacity, political connections, or collusion with officials.
methodology.noteText: Note: HHI here is reported on a 0–1 scale (sum of squares of decimal market shares), where 1.0 represents absolute monopoly. The traditional 0–10,000 scale is divided by 10,000 for ease of comparison.

profile.notFoundDesc: Institution with ID {{id}} could not be located.

risk.benchmarkNote: Vertical line = national average. Rates above average increase corruption risk.

vendors.loyaltyNote: Cells = number of contracts; color = average risk
vendors.efosNote: Cross-reference with EFOS (SAT Art. 69-B) and SFP sanctions available on vendor profiles. Click any vendor to verify external registry status.

officials.methodologyNote: Based on Coviello & Gagliarducci (2017) — official tenure correlates with single-bidder rates. Data available for 2018+ contracts (COMPRANET Structure C/D).
officials.noData: No official data for this institution

external.noAsfData: No ASF audit findings on record
external.crossRegistryNote: External registries: SAT EFOS (Art. 69-B), SFP sanctions, RUPC registry, ASF findings. Check individual vendor profiles for EFOS/SFP status.
